forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRRE6XMJAK3WH3EVMPTMZX8M
+- 01KRRE7WP7YY56R7MTBHJHZD12
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8d80
+project: plugin-arch
+title: 'plugin: callback primitive for plugin→host function references'
+---
+## What
+Implement the single callback primitive the SDK uses wherever a plugin hands the host a function (command `available`/`execute`, view `render`, event handlers, elicitation responses). Functions can't cross the host/plugin boundary directly.
+
+SDK side (`crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/`) + host side (`crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/`):
+- When the SDK dispatches a call whose input contains function values, it assigns each a callback id, stores `{id → fn}` locally in the isolate, and replaces the function in the outgoing payload with `{ "$callback": "cb_xxxx" }`.
+- The host treats `$callback` markers as opaque handles. To invoke one, the host sends `notifications/callbacks/invoke { id, args }` into the isolate.
+- The SDK receives the notification, looks up the stored function, runs it, and — if a return value is expected — sends `notifications/callbacks/result`.
+- This is the ONE primitive behind event subscriptions, command handlers, view renderers, async streaming, and elicitation.
+- Explicitly NOT used for MCP tool calls themselves — those cross via the transports' real MCP JSON-RPC. The primitive is purely plugin→host function references.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Functions in a dispatched payload are replaced with `{$callback: id}` markers; the `{id→fn}` table lives in the isolate.
+- [ ] `notifications/callbacks/invoke` runs the stored function; a return value flows back via `notifications/callbacks/result`.
+- [ ] Tool-call payloads are unaffected — no `$callback` machinery on the transport path.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test through a real isolate: a plugin passes a function to a host-invoked seam; the test triggers `callbacks/invoke` and asserts the function ran and its return value arrived back where it is awaited (observe via a value the plugin writes or returns).
+- [ ] Test a callback that returns a value vs. one that does not — both behave correctly.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the callback round-trip test first, then implement.
+
+## Depends on
+deno_core runtime + TypeScript SDK.
\ No newline at end of file
